observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Treasurer acting as GSO and the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) determine and investigate the reason for the difference of ₱82,938,508.55 between the results of physical inventory of PPE and accounting records; and 



(b) maintain and update the SLCs for each type of PPE and PCs, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of the accounting and property records for reliable reporting of PPE accounts balances in the books.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed to consider the recommendation.





Management committed to consider the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) OIC-MSWDO to exert best efforts to have a regular actual validation/head count of indigent SCs to 

(i) update the master list/ database and 

(ii) ensure that waitlisted SCs who are qualified/ eligible are able to receive monthly Social Pension of ₱500.00 each; and 

(b) OSCA to act on its functions diligently and effectively for the welfare of the SCs.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed to consider the recommendation.

Management committed to consider the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require all the officials responsible to comply with the submission of financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month, pursuant to Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The submission of financial reports and documents still incurred delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) (a) require the MT to deposit collections regularly and intact; (b) provide a service vehicle for use by the MT in depositing collections to the AGDB immediately; and (c) strictly adhere to government rules and regulations in the safekeeping of government funds.

We recommended that the LCE (a) create a Committee on Inventory that will   prioritize the conduct of physical count of the PPE; (b) MA and GSO/MT to (i) avail the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts and prepare a complete Report on Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE); and (ii) derecognize from the books the non-existent PPE; and (c) GSO/MT to maintain bin/stock cards for PPE and PPELC for the MAO for easy reconciliation of PPE records.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to fully implement all the programmed PPAs for the year and  to closely monitor their implementation in accordance with the contract time to enable  the public to use the facilities the soonest possible time; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to observe the requirements of competitive public bidding in the procurement of goods and services pursuant to Section 2 of the 2016 IRR of  R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE require the  (a)  implementing Offices concerned to implement priority projects on the 70% disaster preparedness   fund in a timely manner to achieve disaster avoidance and mitigation of possible impacts of disaster; (b) optimize the utilization of the 30% QRF  on PPAs that will quickly address the needs of the victims of disaster/calamity;  and (c) continue to follow the policy and guidelines on the provision of funds for LDRRM programs, projects and activities to provide maximum care and services to the public in times of calamity.

We recommended that the LCE (a) in the absence of the GAD Focal person, require the Chief of the MPDO to prepare and furnish the AT with GAD Accomplishment Report together with its supporting documents to validate and monitor the expenses charged to the GAD fund; and (b) establish and maintain  the GAD database which will serve as basis for planning, formulating GAD responsive projects, programs and activities.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) MTO to prepare and submit the Report of Checks Issued, Report of Collections and Deposits; paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls and other financial reports to the MAO  within the prescribed period; and (b) MA to strictly comply with the submission of accounts within the first ten days of the ensuing month, pursuant to Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the LCE (a) continuously observe the rules and regulations on disbursements of government funds; and (b) require the MA  to submit  to the AT the aforementioned disbursement vouchers for review and verification.

We recommended that the LCE continuously observe the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and ensure full remittance thereof within the prescribed period to avoid penalty and increase revenues for the government pursuant to BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA to  (i) instruct the concerned personnel to maintain and update subsidiary records and prepare schedules of all GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig members of the Municipality on a monthly basis  for proper monitoring of individual accounts; and (ii) make representation with the corresponding agencies to resolve the discrepancies between the amount of premiums and loan payments deducted with the amounts remitted; and (b) require   the HRMO to submit on time the documents as required by the respective national government agencies mentioned above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) ensure that safekeeping of government funds is prioritized and government rules and regulations are strictly adhered to; and 

(b) consider the provision of service vehicle for use of the Municipal Treasurer in depositing collections to the AGDB immediately upon receipt thereof to comply with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction:
Management committed to consider the recommendation.



The MTO will request a service vehicle.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the immediate liquidation of the cash advance within the prescribed period and to submit the liquidation documents of the cash advance pertaining to the replacement beneficiaries; and 

(b) Head of the MSWD to submit the validated list of beneficiaries.

We commended the Municipality for taking immediate action to provide benefits to its personnel during the pandemic.  We therefore recommended that the Municipality continue its effort in observing rules and regulations concerning payment of allowed benefits to its entitled personnel particularly Hazard Pay and SRA on a timely manner.

We recommended that the LCE require the 

(a) Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fully implement the programmed PPAs under the 20% DF so the public can use the facilities the soonest time possible; and 

(b) Municipal Administrator as the BAC Chairman to ensure that the Bids and Award Committee strictly observe the conduct of public bidding in government procurement.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to consider the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE 

(a) require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation report/s together with the supporting documents for the total cash advance of ₱6,000,000.00; and 

(b) continuously follow the policy guidelines on the provision and utilization of the DRMM funds for the programs, projects and activities to respond to the crisis brought about by COVID-19 virus.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continuously observe the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and the full remittance thereof within the prescribed period to avoid penalty and increase revenues for the government.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant  to 

(a) assign and delegate to  one accounting  staff the maintenance  of  subsidiary records of all GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig members of the Municipality for proper monitoring of individual accounts and subsidiary records and schedules; 

(b) resolve the discrepancies between the amount of premiums and loan repayments deducted with the amounts remitted to GSIS and PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig; and

(c) ensure that the HRMO submits on time the documents as required by the respective government agencies.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office committed to  work on the ledgers.



The Accounting Office  committed to work on the discrepancies.

Management will  work on the recommendation.




Projects will be completed soon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure that safekeeping of government funds is prioritized and that government rules and regulations are strictly adhered to; and (b) consider the provision of service vehicle for use of the Municipal Treasurer in depositing collections to the AGDB immediately upon receipt thereof in order to comply with existing regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the immediate liquidation of the cash advance within the prescribed period and to submit the liquidation documents of the cash advance pertaining to the replacement beneficiaries; and (b) Head of the MSWD to submit the validated list of beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer acting as GSO and the Municipal Accountant to (a) determine and investigate the reason for the difference of ₱82,938,508.55 between the results of physical inventory of PPE and accounting records; and (b) maintain and update the SLCs for each type of PPE and PCs, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of the accounting and property records for reliable reporting of PPE accounts balances in the books.

We therefore recommended that the LCE continue his efforts in observing rules and regulations concerning payment of allowed benefits to its entitled personnel particularly Hazard Pay and SRA on a timely manner.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the (a) OIC-MSWDO to exert best efforts to have a regular actual validation/head count of indigent SCs to (i) update the masterlist/database; and (ii) ensure that waitlisted SCs who are qualified/eligible are able to receive monthly Social Pension of ₱500.00 each; and (b) OSCA to act on its functions diligently and effectively for the welfare of the SCs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to fully implement the programmed PPAs under the 20% DF so the public can use the facilities the soonest time possible; and (b) Municipal Administrator as the BAC Chairman to ensure that the Bids and Award Committee strictly observe the conduct of public bidding  in government procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Audit Team  the liquidation report/s together with the supporting documents for the total cash advance of ₱6,000,000.00; and (b) continuously follow the policy guidelines on the provision and utilization of the DRMM funds for the programs, projects and activities to respond to the crisis brought about by COVID-19 virus.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to (a)strictly observe   the provisions under JMC No. 2013-01 as amended by JMC No. 2016-01 which provides the Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women;  (b) ensure the early preparation and submission of the GPB to DILG for review/approval and thereafter, for endorsement to  PCW; (c) establish and submit to the Audit Team the details of accomplishment reports together with their supporting documents to validate the expenses charged to the GAD fund;(d) monitor the GAD accomplishments based on the approved GPB and submit  to DILG on a timely basis;(e) set up and maintain the GAD database which will serve as the basis for planning, formulation of  policies and identification of  GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; and  (f) recommend the formulation of a  GAD Code to support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the officials responsible to comply with the submission of financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month, pursuant to Section 347 of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to continuously observe the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and the full remittance thereof within the prescribed period to avoid penalty and increase revenues for the government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant  to (a) assign and delegate to  one accounting  staff the maintenance  of  subsidiary records of all GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig members of the Municipality for proper monitoring of individual accounts and subsidiary records and schedules; (b) resolve the discrepancies between the amount of premiums and loan repayments deducted with the amounts remitted to GSIS and PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig; (c) ensure that the HRMO submits on time the documents as required by the respective government agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the 

(a) Municipal Administrator to facilitate the submission to the Office of the COA Chairperson through the ICFAU, of the liquidation documents for the Cash Advance granted for Confidential Fund amounting to ₱1,997,000.00 and 

(b) Municipal Accountant to 

(i) ensure that supporting documents are complete and in the required form;  

(ii) record the corresponding liquidation upon receipt of liquidation documents with the Transmittal Letter duly stamped received by ICFAU; and 

(iii) make the necessary adjusting entries to restore the Cash Advances to the affected accounts to correct the understatement of the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and overstatement of the Confidential Expenses for the same amount of ₱1,997,000.00.

We recommended that the LCE require the head of the MDRRMO 

(a) to strictly implement the guidelines in the allocation and utilization of the projects in the AIP using LDRRMF; 

(b) properly plan and program appropriate PPAs for inclusion to the AIP and refrain from charging improper and unrelated expenditures against the fund; and 

(c) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to properly acknowledge all   donations for calamity victims from any sources whether in cash or in kind for proper acknowledgment and accounting.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineering Office to 

(a) prepare periodic status reports/maintain records on the status or extent of accomplishment of development projects being implemented by the Municipality with complete essential information and submit to the Audit Team for review; and 

(b) closely supervise the implementation of the remaining projects funded under 20% DF for maximum utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to observe BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period to avoid penalty that may be imposed by BIR.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) instruct the concerned accounting personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig members for proper close monitoring of the correct balances of individual member accounts; 

(b) conduct further verification of accounts with over and under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment in the books and subsidiary records of members; and 

(c) make representations with the GSIS concerning their system of receiving remittances in order to resolve the noted discrepancies.

(b) Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the submission of accounts and financial reports within the set deadline under Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 date September 15, 2009 and Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor 

(a) require the Municipal Engineering Office to prepare periodic status reports/ maintain records on the status or extent of accomplishment of development projects being implemented by the Municipality with complete essential information and submit to the COA Auditor’s Office for review; 

(b) augment the MPDO and MEO with personnel possessing technical qualifications to strengthen the monitoring of projects; and 

(c) require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to ensure the completeness of the documents attached to the DV before payment is made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to 

(a) include in the budget the provision for adequate, effective and comfortable personal protective equipment to garbage collectors and MRF workers to protect them from the hazards of handling wastes; and 

(b) evaluate the working conditions of workers at the MRF and at the field and provide them protection from occupational work hazards such as falls and injury while in transit.
observations.managementAction: Provision for PPE will be considered in next budget year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that MENRO see to it that the waste compartment of all garbage trucks will have cover to ensure the containment of the solid wastes while in transit and inform all personnel assigned on the roads to monitor violations of the prohibited acts.
observations.managementAction: This was not given adequate attention.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) Municipal Administrator to facilitate the submission to the Office of the COA Chairperson through the ICFAU, of the liquidation documents for the Cash Advance granted for Confidential Fund amounting to ₱1,997,000.00 and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that supporting documents are complete and in the required form; and (ii)record the corresponding liquidation upon receipt of liquidation documents with the Transmittal Letter duly stamped received by ICFAU;  and (iii) make the necessary adjusting entries to restore  the Cash Advances to the affected accounts to correct the understatement of the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and overstatement of the Confidential Expenses for the same amount of ₱1,997,000.00.
According to Management, appropriate action will be taken to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant  to (i) closely coordinate with the LBP for the immediate notification of  downloaded funds by the bank to the Municipal Officials; (ii) immediately inform the respective DBM officials of the Municipality’s Land Bank of the Philippines – Trust Fund (LBP TF) account number for future downloading of funds intended for specific projects; (b)  Municipal Engineer to take the initiative to inform the concerned Procurement Unit  and the Bids and Awards Committee on the timetable of project procurement and implementation and provide the list of projects with corresponding funding sources; (c) Municipal DRRMO to plan and monitor the progress of  accomplishment of the projects; and  (d) Municipal Administrator to (i) assign an official in charge of projects funded by external sources for strict monitoring of implementation schedules and targets;  and (ii) ensure that the fund proponent is informed on the project status including the liquidation reports and related documents; and (ii) see to it that, henceforth,  all LGSF projects will be  completed at the earliest possible time.
The observations were well-noted by Management for compliance and guidance in its operation.

We recommended that the LCE require the head of the MDRRMO (a) to strictly  implement the guidelines in  the allocation and utilization  of the projects in the AIP using LDRRMF; (b) properly plan and program appropriate PPAs for inclusion to the AIP and refrain from charging improper and unrelated expenditures against  the fund; and (c) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to properly acknowledge all   donations for calamity victims from any sources whether in cash or in kind for proper acknowledgment and accounting.
Management agreed to implement the recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineering Office to (a) prepare periodic status reports/maintain records on the status or extent of accomplishment of development projects being implemented by the Municipality with complete essential information and submit to the Audit Team for review; and (b) closely supervise the implementation of the remaining projects funded under 20% DF for maximum utilization of the fund.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE require the GAD Focal Person to: (i)  ensure the early preparation and submission of the GPB to DILG for review/approval and thereafter for endorsement to  PCW; (ii)  set up and maintain the GAD database which will serve as the basis for planning, formulation of  policies and identification of  GAD responsive projects, programs and activities and strict observance of  the provisions under JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01; and (iii) recommend the formulation of a  GAD Code to support the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment; (iv) monitor  the GAD accomplishments based on the approved GPB and submit accordingly  to the DILG on a timely basis.
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended that the LCE require the head of the MENRO to (a) closely monitor the establishment of MRF in the barangays to fully comply with the law; (b) strictly implement the segregation of wastes at source and impose appropriate sanctions; (c) include in the budget the provision for adequate, effective and comfortable personal protective equipment to garbage collectors and MRF workers to protect them from the hazards of handling wastes; (d) evaluate the working conditions of workers at the MRF and at the field and provide them protection from occupational work hazards while in transit; (e) provide  the waste compartment of all garbage trucks with adequate cover to ensure the containment of  the solid wastes while in transit to prevent spillage.
The Head of MENRO informed the AT that these matters will be considered for appropriate action.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to observe BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof within the prescribed period to avoid penalty that may be imposed by BIR.
The Municipal Accountant noted the recommendations for compliance.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a) instruct the concerned accounting personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-Ibig members for proper close monitoring of the correct  balances of individual member accounts; (b) conduct further verification of accounts with over and under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment in the books and subsidiary records of members; and (c) make representations  with the GSIS concerning their  system of receiving remittances in order  to resolve the noted discrepancies.
The Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor 

(a) refrain from granting cash advances accommodating other participants’ travel/ seminar expenses and activities through cash advances granted in the name of elected officials; 

(b) grant cash advances to elected officials for their own official travel expenses only; and 

(c) require the Municipal Accountant to draw JEVs for the adjustment of the noted errors in recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant 

(a) to strictly observe the rules and regulations pertaining to LDRRMF particularly the monitoring of cash receipts from donations intended for disaster victims to be deposited to the Trust Fund account for DRRMF and ensure timely recording in the Trust Fund (TF) books using the account Trust Liabilities-DRRMF; and 

(b) make the necessary adjustment to correct the entry made for the donation recorded in Due to LGU account.

We recommended that the

(a) Municipal Administrator expedite the submission of the liquidation documents for the cash advance granted as Confidential Fund to the Office of the Chairperson through the ICFAU; and

(b) Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents for this type of cash advance are complete and in the required form and record the liquidation only upon receipt of liquidation documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer and the head of the Economic Enterprise to 

(i) determine the correct amount of collectibles from market stallholders; and 

(ii) closely coordinate  the enforcement of the Market Code of 2010;  and 

(b)  Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books to reflect the additional receivable account and see to it that the recognition of financial information is in accordance with IPSAS to reflect a fair presentation of the financial statements and be useful to users and stakeholders.

(b) Municipal General Services Office to coordinate with MAO for proper documentation of disposal and submit to MAO the supporting documents necessary for the derecognition of the assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to 

(a) strictly enforce anti-littering ordinances, create anti -littering brigades in barangays and other strategies to counteract the uncontrolled dumping of wastes along main roads and waterways; and 

(b) post additional signboards for the schedule of garbage collection per type of wastes.

observations.recommendation: 1.5    We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) refrain from granting cash advances accommodating other participants’ travel/seminar expenses and activities through cash advances granted in the  name of elected officials; (b) grant cash advances  to elected officials for their  own official travel expenses only; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to draw JEVs for the adjustment of the noted errors in recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant (a) to strictly observe the rules and regulations pertaining to LDRRMF particularly the monitoring of cash receipts from donations intended for disaster victims to be deposited to the Trust Fund account for DRRMF and ensure timely recording in the Trust Fund (TF) books using the account Trust Liabilities-DRRMF; and (b) make the necessary adjustment to correct the entry made for the donation recorded in Due to LGU account.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Administrator expedite the submission of the liquidation documents for the cash advance granted as Confidential Fund to the Office of the Chairperson through the ICFAU; and (b) Municipal Accountant ensure that supporting documents for this type of cash advance are complete and in the required form and record the liquidation only upon receipt of liquidation documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Municipal Treasurer and the head of the Economic Enterprise to (i) determine the correct amount of collectibles from market stallholders; and (ii) closely coordinate  the enforcement of the Market Code of 2010;  and (b)  Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books to reflect the additional receivable account and see to it that the recognition of financial information is in accordance with PPSAS to reflect a fair presentation of the financial statements and be useful to users and stakeholders.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) Municipal Accounting Office (MAO) to record in the books the disposed assets based on reported Net Book Value together with the amount of loss on sale of assets; and (b) Municipal General Services Office to coordinate with MAO for proper documentation of disposal and submit to MAO the supporting documents necessary for the derecognition of the assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the procured Backhoe Crawler type under the account Construction and Heavy Equipment instead of Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to record the property under the Watercraft account for a fair presentation of the financial statements at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Engineering Office to prepare periodic status reports/maintain records on the status or extent of accomplishment of development projects being implemented by the Municipality with complete essential information and submit to the COA Auditor’s Office for review; (b) augment the MPDO and MEO with personnel possessing technical qualifications to strengthen the monitoring of projects; and (c)  require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate  with the Municipal Engineer to ensure the completeness of the documents attached to the DV before payment is made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) consider constructing  a central storage area/stockroom to house all deliveries of supplies; (b) require the property/supply custodians to (i) make use of RIS for every withdrawal/issuance of supplies and submit to Accounting Office the MRSI ; (ii) submit the inventory report on supplies every semester while the Property and Equipment inventory count report at year-end on or before January 31 of the following year; and (iii)  maintain stock cards/subsidiary records for individual items on stock.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer to (a) strictly enforce anti-littering ordinances, create anti -littering brigades in barangays and other strategies to counteract the uncontrolled dumping of wastes along main roads and waterways; and (b) post additional signboards for the schedule of garbage collection per type of wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to (a) include in the budget the provision for adequate, effective and comfortable personal protective equipment to garbage collectors and MRF workers to protect them from the hazards of handling wastes; and (b) evaluate the working conditions of workers at the MRF and at the field and provide them protection from occupational work hazards such as falls and injury while in transit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the head of MENRO to (a) strictly implement wastes segregation at source; (b) consider the application of appropriate sanctions if sorting by household is not done; (c) closely monitor the solid waste practices of barangays without MRF; and (d) limit the expenditures for hauling by setting a certain quota or limit of truckload per day for each barangay depending on the existing population and livelihood.

We recommended that MENRO see to it that the waste compartment of all garbage trucks will have cover to ensure the containment of the solid wastes while in transit and inform all personnel assigned on the roads to monitor violations of the prohibited acts.
We recommended that the MENRO provide in its ensuing budget an allocation for a suitable equipment that could be useful, efficient and effective in solid waste management.
We recommended that the Head of the DRRMO closely monitor the implementation of the projects in the AIP using DRRMF and refrain from charging unrelated expenditures to the DRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) GAD Focal Person to: (i) strictly observe the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01, and its amendments under JMC No. 2016-01 which provides for the Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women to facilitate timely submission; (ii) establish  GAD database to serve as the basis for planning, formulating policies and identifying GAD responsive projects, programs and activities; (iii) submit the details of expenses charged to GAD fund for validation purposes and (b) assign  personnel to monitor the required schedule in the submission of the GAD Plan and Budget to DILG for review and approval and likewise, the submission of  GPB and AR  to COA  under item V of  COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the head of the Economic Enterprise to strictly impose the stipulations in the Market Code of 2010; (b) require the members of the Market Committee to perform their duties to improve the operation of the market; and (c) apply all measures and techniques to collect payments from the delinquent stallholders and those with arrears in the public cemetery to pay their unpaid accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BUB Focal Person to ensure full implementation of the remaining BUB projects as envisioned under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the MSWDO to (i) conduct regular validation of SCs to be used as a database in the grant of Social Pension to qualified and eligible SCs such as  the  indigent SCs, Persons with disability and sick with incurable illness; (ii) consider inclusion of thumbmarks of SCs and  SC’s data on siblings  on the application form for membership; (iii) coordinate  with NPO to come up with the database of the qualified/priority indigent SC beneficiaries; and (iv) validate the list of SCs eligible for the Social Pension Program before submission to DSWDO to determine the present health condition of SCs if needing replacement so other SCs can benefit  from the Program and available funds could be fully utilized.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for welfare goods for consumption and for distribution to closely monitor the movement of the accounts; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer are acting GSO to prepare and maintain the stock cards for welfare goods for stock or for distribution, as the case maybe, on a per item basis or subsidiary ledgers or its equivalent record to properly monitor the correctness of the inventory accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit the financial reports and documents within the first ten days of the ensuing month; (ii) ensure that reports and supporting documents were signed immediately by the approving officials to avoid unnecessary delay; and (b) consider augmenting the personnel complement in the Accounting Office in order to comply with the timely submission of accounts requirement.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from granting cash incentives to non-qualified personnel without legal basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) strictly observe the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof by remitting in full the taxes actually collected by the Municipality within the prescribed period to avoid any penalty; and (b) continue to follow BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof on a monthly basis within the prescribed period to avoid penalty that may be imposed by BIR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) instruct the concerned personnel to maintain schedules and subsidiary records of all GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG members of the Municipality for proper monitoring of individual accounts; (ii) resolve the discrepancies between the amount of premiums and loan payments deducted with the amount remitted to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG; and (b) ensure that the HRMO submits on time the required documents needed by the NGAs.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to 

a) analyze the bank and book reconciling items and make the necessary adjustment on valid reconciling items; and 

b) submit for audit the Bank Reconciliation Statements from October to December 2017 and prepare/submit the BRS on a regular basis.

We recommended that Management 

a) submit to the Audit Team the case studies pertaining to the beneficiaries that served as basis for the cash advance granted to the Municipal Treasurer for payment of FA to individuals in crisis situation;

b) refrain from granting another cash advance unless the previous cash advance is first settled; and 

c) submit to the Audit Team the MOA with DSWD on the livelihood project to determine the conditions for the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management require the: 

a) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all PPE and expedite the submission of the physical inventory report not later than January 31 of each year; and 


b) Require the MTO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Accounting Books and Property Records and to maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for easy reconciliation of records to settle the discrepancy noted.
observations.managementAction:
Management will comply with the recommendation.


Management will comply with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
for lack of personnel, there were delays in submission of report.

Not Implemented, for lack of subsidiary for PPE at Accounting office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the immediate transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to the appropriate special trust fund account and maintain subsidiary ledgers.

Management will exert best effort to implement BUB projects as planned.


Management will return the unused funds to NGAs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant:  

a) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects as required by COA Circular 2012-001, the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport and the IRR of RA No. 9184 by submitting the Original and As Built Plans, testing results for materials used, PERT/CPM, and logbook of project activities and other required documents; and 
 
b) in coordination with the Bids and Award Committee to submit the print out copy of the posting made to PhilGEPS for the bidded/awarded projects and submit the original documents to support the paid vouchers of the implemented projects.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert best effort to comply with recommendation.
observations.reason: some documents were attached to disbursements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management through the Local School Board to 

a) strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160, JMC No. 1 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG on the proper utilization of the SEF for its intended purpose only; 

b) stop the practice of granting cash advances to DepEd officials/employees who are non-organic employees of the Municipality; and 

c) Consider payment of expenses directly to payees instead of through reimbursement.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management 

a) impose all legal remedies under R.A. No. 7160 to compel delinquent OPM tenants to settle their unpaid accounts and adopt a collection strategy whereby the stallholders would be encouraged to settle their accounts; and 

b) Instruct the members of the Market Committee to hold meeting with the market tenants and devise more effective and efficient income generating measures for the operation of all economic enterprises.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert best efforts to locate deliquent tenants.
observations.reason: Not Implemented, for lack of information on tenants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to continue to observe BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof in full within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the concerned personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all PAG-IBIG members of the Municipality for proper monitoring of accounts and to verify further and make the necessary adjustments to those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance.
.

We recommended that Management require the 

a) Municipal Human Relations Officer to inform all PhilHealth members to secure and submit the ID Numbers; and 

b) Municipal Accountant to verify those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment.

(a) intensify the reconciliation of the PPE records in order to arrive at the correct balances of PPE accounts in the Financial Statements 

(b) instruct the GSO to maintain Property Cards for each item of PPE to support the details of the reported inventory; 
(c) instruct the Inventory Committee to exert effort in obtaining documents to prove the true ownership of the 29 lots not registered under the name of the Municipality to pave the way for their registration  and

(d) exert extra effort to retrieve the documents to support the disposal of unserviceable properties to save them from further deterioration and reduction in value.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to (a) analyze the bank and book reconciling items and make the necessary adjustment on valid reconciling items; and (b) submit for audit the Bank Reconciliation Statements from October to December 2017 and prepare/submit the BRS on a regular basis.

We recommended that Management (a) submit to the Audit Team the case studies pertaining to the beneficiaries that served as basis for the cash advance granted to the Municipal Treasurer for payment of FA to individuals in crisis situation;(b) refrain from granting another cash advance unless the previous cash advance is first settled; and (c) submit to the Audit Team the MOA with DSWD on the livelihood project to determine the conditions for the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to gather and submit all available documents necessary for the filing of the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts to the COA Auditor’s Office for a fair presentation of the Receivable account in the FS.

We recommended that Management require the: (a) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of all PPE and expedite the submission of the physical inventory report not later than January 31 of each year; and (b) require the MTO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Accounting Books and Property Records and to maintain Property Cards and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for easy reconciliation of records to settle the discrepancy noted.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor the immediate transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to the appropriate special trust fund account and maintain subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that Management require the: a) BUB Focal Person to strictly oversee the timely implementation of all planned programs and projects;    b) BAC officials to ensure that the voucher for payment is supported with the original copies of the required documents;  c) Municipal Engineer to submit the  original technical documents mentioned above; and d) Municipal Accountant to  return to the Bureau of Treasury  the long outstanding balance of the Due to NGAS account with completed projects/programs and to the source agencies  the unspent fund balances of BUB projects with identified government agencies.

We recommended that Management require the: a) BUB Focal Person to strictly oversee the timely implementation of all planned programs and projects;    b) BAC officials to ensure that the voucher for payment is supported with the original copies of the required documents;  c) Municipal Engineer to submit the  original technical documents mentioned above; and d) Municipal Accountant to  return to the Bureau of Treasury  the long outstanding balance of the Due to NGAS account with completed projects/programs and to the source agencies  the unspent fund balances of BUB projects with identified government agencies.

We recommended that Management require the: a) BUB Focal Person to strictly oversee the timely implementation of all planned programs and projects;    b) BAC officials to ensure that the voucher for payment is supported with the original copies of the required documents;  c) Municipal Engineer to submit the  original technical documents mentioned above; and d) Municipal Accountant to  return to the Bureau of Treasury  the long outstanding balance of the Due to NGAS account with completed projects/programs and to the source agencies  the unspent fund balances of BUB projects with identified government agencies.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant:  a) in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to ensure  the  completeness of the documentary requirements for infrastructure projects as required by COA Circular 2012-001, the DPWH Standard Specifications for Highways, Bridges and Airport and the IRR of RA No. 9184 by submitting the Original and As Built Plans, testing results for materials used, PERT/CPM, and logbook of project activities and other required documents; and b) in coordination with the Bids and Award Committee to submit the print out copy of the posting made to PhilGEPS for the bidded/awarded projects and submit the original documents to support the paid vouchers of the implemented projects.

We recommended that Management through the Local School Board to a) strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160, JMC No. 1 s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG on the proper utilization of the SEF for its intended purpose only; b) stop the practice of granting cash advances to DepEd officials/employees who are non-organic employees of the Municipality; and b) consider payment of expenses directly to payees instead of through reimbursement.
We recommended that Management require the MENRO to prioritize the conduct of waste characterization studies for integration to the SWMP from CY 2017 to 2026.

We recommended that Management require the barangays without MRF to locate a suitable space for establishing their MRF so the residual wastes will be minimized hence, reduce the final disposal fee being paid by the Municipality.
We recommended that Management require the MENRO to provide the waste compartment of all garbage trucks with cover to ensure the containment of the solid wastes while in transit.

We recommended that the a) MSWDO conduct actual validation of the families and individuals in the affected barangays to come up with the victim profiles/database; and b) submit to MDRRMO the estimated provision for data base generation of disaster victims, stockpile of relief goods for inclusion in the AIP for DRRMF.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that Management (a) impose all legal remedies under R.A. No. 7160 to compel delinquent OPM tenants to settle their unpaid accounts and adopt a collection strategy whereby the stallholders would be encouraged to settle their accounts; and (b) instruct the members of the Market Committee to hold meeting with the market tenants and devise more effective and efficient income generating measures for the operation of all economic enterprises.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to continue to observe BIR rules and regulations on the withholding of taxes and remittance thereof in full within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require the a) Municipal Human Relations Officer to inform all GSIS members of the Municipality to secure and submit the GSIS ID number; b) Municipal Accountant to verify and make the necessary adjustments to those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the concerned personnel to maintain subsidiary records of all PAG-IBIG members of the Municipality for proper monitoring of accounts and to verify further and make the necessary adjustments to those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance.

We recommended that Management require the a) Municipal Human Relations Officer to inform all PhilHealth members to secure and submit the ID Numbers; and b) Municipal Accountant to verify those accounts with over or under deduction or remittance and make the necessary adjustment.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to (a) set up and maintain the GAD database and assists in the formulation of the GAD Code to support the LGU’s efforts in promoting gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment; and (b) monitor the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget to DILG for review and approval and monitor the accomplishments vis a vis the plan.

(a) direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer in establishing the correct amount of basic RPT and SET receivables; and 

(b) regularly reconcile the amount among the concerned Offices in order to arrive at the correct balance of the accounts.

Conduct meeting with officials re: Solid Waste Management Plan

observations.recommendation:
(a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities/programs for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and 

(b) maximize utilization of SEF for the attainment of its primary goals and objectives and ensure that PPAs are in accordance with DepEd criteria and the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.

observations.recommendation: refrain from using the 30% Quick Response Fund of the LDRRMF for expenses which are not in accordance with the intent of the fund utilization to avoid incurrence of irregular expenses for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Conduct meeting to discuss the utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation: exert more effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Conduct meeting among agency officials.

observations.recommendation: continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period and resolve the noted discrepancy for necessary adjustment in the books of accounts
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 

properly observe the guidelines in the granting and utilization of the Confidential Fund in accordance with Joint Circular No. 1 dated January 8, 2015; 

(b) submit the cash advances vouchers to the audit team together with the supporting documents in the manner prescribed by the Joint Circular; and 

(c) submit to Office of the COA Chairperson thru the ICFAU.  The liquidation reports covering expenses for intelligence fund for CY 2015 for post audit and evaluation in order that credits to said cash advances be made.

We recommended that Management 

(a) instruct the MENRO to conduct massive campaign on all barangays concerned to   residents/households towards a full implementation of the basic rules on waste segregation to reduce service costs; 

(b) strictly enforce among the residents the No littering policy and No segregation/no collection rule; and 

(c) exert more effort in complying with environmental laws by informing the citizenry of the consequences for the non-compliance to the laws.

We recommended that management 

(a) strictly comply with the provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the proper use of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to avoid incurrence of unnecessary and irregular expenses and 

(b) submit the necessary documents before utilizing the fund to substantiate the documents evidencing the afore-cited disbursements.

We recommended that Management 

(a) strengthen its GFPS thru capacity building and coordination with other offices of the Municipality and other stakeholders;  

(b)  comply with the essential elements of GAD Planning and Budgeting to be able to identify, prioritize and address gender issues and GAD mandates; and 

(c) require submission of GAD Accomplishment Report which is in accordance with reportorial contents to facilitate evaluation and to easily determine whether activities undertaken had addressed the identified gender issues.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period and resolve the noted discrepancy for necessary adjustment in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management (a) intensify the reconciliation of the PPE records in order to arrive at the correct balances of PPE accounts in the Financial Statements (b) instruct the GSO to maintain Property Cards for each item of PPE to support the details of the reported inventory; (c) instruct the Inventory Committee to exert effort in obtaining documents to prove the true ownership of the 29 lots not registered under the name of the Municipality to pave the way for their registration  and  (d) exert extra effort to retrieve the documents to support the disposal of unserviceable properties to save them from further deterioration and reduction in value.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer in establishing the correct amount of basic RPT and SET receivables; and (b) regularly reconcile the amount among the concerned Offices in order to arrive at the correct balance of the accounts.

We recommended that Management (a) strictly observe the guidelines in the granting and utilization of the Confidential Fund in accordance with Joint Circular No. 1 dated January 8, 2015; (b) submit the cash advance vouchers to the audit team together with the supporting documents in the manner prescribed by the Joint Circular; and (c) submit to Office of the COA Chairperson thru the ICFAU, the liquidation reports covering expenses for intelligence fund for CY 2016 for post audit and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require (a) the submission of the pertinent documents in compliance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012, and (b) the attachment of the same required documents in the succeeding transactions together with copies of trip tickets for verification and reconciliation purposes.

We recommended that Management (a) consider adopting the DSWD regulation relative to the grant of financial assistance; and (b) request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution establishing a set of specific guidelines, in harmony with DSWD policies, on the grant of financial assistance/aid to rationalize disbursements under the Other MOOE account.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Accountant (a) to submit the necessary documents supporting the BUB transactions pursuant to COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to facilitate audit and verification thereof; (b) to effect the necessary corrections in the recording of the transactions to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management (a) maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and implement all programmed development projects for the benefit of their constituents; and (b) plan and strictly implement all projects in accordance with the approved Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Solid Waste Management Board to cause the speedy preparation of the City Solid Waste  Management Plan and its supporting documents as their basis in the effective implementation of solid waste management; (b) provide necessary support in the establishment of MRFs at the Barangays; (c) cause an efficient and safe operation in the barangay MRFs through proper segregation of wastes and in the Municipality’s dumping station through provision of a fence to prevent hazards to the environment; d) encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; and (e) recommend to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that Management (a) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities/programs for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (b) maximize utilization of SEF for the attainment of its primary goals and objectives and ensure that PPAs are in accordance with DepEd criteria and the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.

We recommended that Management refrain from using the 30% Quick Response Fund of the LDRRMF for expenses which are not in accordance with the intent of the fund utilization to avoid incurrence of irregular expenses for the Municipality.
We recommended that Management exert more effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period and resolve the noted discrepancy for necessary adjustment in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management 

(a) strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and 

(b) submit to the COA Audit Team the original copy of the disbursement vouchers representing cash advances for travels to South Korea and New Zealand of the Municipal Mayor together with the liquidation reports thereof for post audit.
observations.managementAction:
The management  strictly adhered to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Has  submitted to the COA Audit Team  the original copy of the disbursement vouchers representing cash advances for  travels to South Korea and New Zealand of the Municipal Mayor together with  the liquidation reports thereof for post audit.

Accounting Office started to maintain the PPELC.

The GSO already submitted the request for disposal to COA and the report was already transmitted, however, the unserviceable properties were not yet disposed as of this date. 
(Reiterated under Observation No. 1 of the 2016 AAR.)
The Accounting Office started to prepare reports financial statements for their economic enterprises.
Demand letters have been sent to the stallholders to settle their accounts and conducted meeting to discuss the problems affecting their leased stalls.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management 

(a) refrain from using the unexpended balances of the 20% economic development fund without first reverting it to unappropriated surplus of the general fund; and 

(b) direct the MPDO and Budget Office to request for re appropriation of the fund balance before utilizing the same.
observations.managementAction: Balances of the 20% EDF were reverted to unappropriated surplus and requested SB for its re-appropriation.
